title: Crispy Nostalgic Saudia Sesame Stir-fry
description: A flavorful and crispy dish featuring sesame butter with a touch of umami, perfect for those looking to enjoy a nostalgic taste of Saudi Arabia while following a fodmap-friendly diet.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: regular soy sauce is recommended for umami flavor

ingredients.name: ginger puree
ingredients.notes: freshly grated ginger can be used instead

ingredients.name: oil
ingredients.notes: extra virgin olive oil is recommended for crispiness

ingredients.name: soaked vermicelli noodles
ingredients.notes: adjust quantity based on serving size

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together sesame seeds with palm sugar and soy sauce. If using chili flakes, add them now as well.
Stir in ginger puree, garlic paste, and soaked vermicelli noodles until well combined. Adjust seasoning to taste if needed.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Once hot, pour the sesame seed mixture into the pan and cook for about 5 minutes or until the sauce thickens and coats the noodles evenly.
Transfer the stir-fried dish onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake at 40°C (320°F) for 1 hour. Flip occasionally to ensure even cooking.
Remove from oven once golden brown and crispy, then return to skillet to keep warm before serving.
